model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Nebraska holds a commanding 125+ spot advantage in advanced metrics with an elite KenPom ranking of 14th and the nation's 7th-ranked defense. The Cornhuskers' +26.12 adjusted efficiency margin dwarfs Troy's +1.74 mark, presenting a severe mismatch that history suggests is insurmountable for a 13-seed. While Troy captured the Sun Belt championship and played competitively against power conference opponents, their statistical profile lacks the firepower to overcome Nebraska's Big Ten-tested roster and balanced two-way excellence.

reasoningStep.title: Nebraska vs Troy - 4/13 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Nebraska enters with elite national metrics (KenPom #14, Torvik #19) featuring a top-10 defense and +26.12 efficiency margin, creating a massive 125+ ranking spot gap over Troy (KenPom #143). The Cornhuskers' Big Ten-tested resume with quality wins over Illinois, Michigan State, and Purdue provides significant structural advantages over the Sun Belt champions. While Troy demonstrated competitiveness in close losses to power conference opponents, their +1.74 efficiency margin and porous defense present no credible path to overcome Nebraska's balanced excellence at the historically upset-prone 4/13 line.
evidence: Nebraska KenPom rank: 14th, Torvik rank: 19th, efficiency margin: +26.12
Nebraska defense ranked 7th on Torvik, 92.5 on KenPom (elite)
Troy KenPom rank: 143rd, Torvik rank: 142nd, efficiency margin: +1.74
Troy offense ranked 141st on KenPom vs Nebraska defense 7th on Torvik = severe mismatch
Nebraska record 26-6 (2nd in Big Ten) with wins over Illinois, Michigan State, Purdue, Creighton
Troy record 22-11 with close losses to USC (107-106) and San Diego State (108-107)

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
Now I have some useful information:

Nebraska:
- 26-6 record, 2nd in Big Ten
- Notable wins: @ Illinois, @ Ohio State, vs Michigan State, vs Purdue, vs Creighton, vs Wisconsin
- Notable losses: @ Michigan, vs Illinois, vs Purdue, @ Iowa, @ UCLA
- They played a very tough Big Ten schedule and had a strong season

Troy:
- 22-11 record, 1st in Sun Belt
- Notable non-conference: Lost at USC (107-106), Lost at San Diego State (108-107 - close game!), Lost at Kent State
- Some decent wins but mostly against Sun Belt competition
- Their non-conference performance shows they can compete with power conference teams (close losses to USC and San Diego State)

The statistical gap is still enormous - about 125 spots in the rankings with a 24+ point efficiency margin difference. Nebraska's elite defense (7th nationally) against Troy's mediocre offense (141st) is a huge mismatch. Troy's defense (181st) against Nebraska's offense (55th) is also problematic.

This is a 4/13 matchup where 13 seeds upset about 20% of the time historically. The statistical profile strongly favors Nebraska, and I don't have any information about injuries that would change this.

I'll pick Nebraska with high confidence given the massive statistical advantage and power conference pedigree.

Now I have a comprehensive view of this matchup. Let me compile my findings:

**Key Findings:**
- **Nebraska**: KenPom #14, Torvik #19, 26-6 record (2nd in Big Ten), elite defense (7th on Torvik), +26.12 efficiency margin
- **Troy**: KenPom #143, Torvik #142, 22-11 record (Sun Belt champions), mediocre defense (181st), +1.74 efficiency margin
- **Gap**: Approximately 125-130 ranking spots with a 24+ point efficiency margin differential
- **Matchup History**: This is a 4/13 matchup where 13 seeds upset about 20% historically
- **Quality wins**: Nebraska beat Illinois, Michigan State, Purdue, Creighton, and Wisconsin; Troy played competitively against USC and San Diego State but lost both

The statistical profile heavily favors Nebraska with an elite defense that should suffocate Troy's mediocre offense, while Nebraska's efficient offense should exploit Troy's porous defense. Troy showed fight in close losses to power conference teams, but the efficiency gap is too substantial to justify an upset pick.
